main_useful_patterns: partial_progress trajectories exist and should be preserved or improved
success_like trajectories exist and should be strongly preferred
uncertainties: Do not construct success_like preference pairs because no high-confidence success_like trajectory exists.

forbidden_changes: do not only scale all coefficients
do not add global survival bonus without progress gating
do not treat noisy partial_progress as success_like
required_changes: explicitly avoid known failures: early_failure, low_progress_survival
preserve useful patterns: partial_progress trajectories exist and should be preserved or improved; success_like trajectories exist and should be strongly preferred
gate or remove components: low_progress_survival_gate, stability_near, terminal_reward, ungated_contact_bonus, ungated_survival_bonus

preserve or strengthen components: approach_progress, far_approach, far_quality_penalty, leg_bonus, low_progress_penalty, near_stability, proximity_bonus, terminal_bonus, terminal_failure_penalty, terminal_penalty

rollback_decision.reason: Hard rollback is handled by RollbackManager, not Analyzer.

self_evaluation_lesson: Current memory mainly supports avoiding known failures, not claiming success.

previous_selection_used.allowed_decision: Do not use Memory-TAGE as a strong selector; prefer conservative/elite-preserving mutation.

candidate_scores.allowed_decision: Do not use Memory-TAGE as a strong selector; prefer conservative/elite-preserving mutation.
Do not use Memory-TAGE as a strong selector; prefer conservative/elite-preserving mutation.

previous_selection_used.reason: Memory coverage has no selection authority; selected conservative valid candidate using static/novelty prior.

recent_failure_memory_used.lesson: Partial progress signals remain unreliable due to tiny label margins; conservative use of memory is required even when training return improves slightly.
future_guidance: continue to preserve progress conditioning and approach_progress
strengthen terminal_bonus to be achievable with moderate speeds and both legs contact
restructure terminal_penalty to clearly separate crash from safe landing and ambiguous termination
avoid constructing preference pairs from weak memory until high-confidence success_like trajectories exist
consider rollback to best elite if training return declines again

archive_update.reason: Selected candidate improved training return and produced first success-like trajectory but still has insufficient label margins and lower static score than best elite; not added to elite archive.

uncertainties: Label margin is insufficient (progress gap 0.017 vs threshold 0.05) to treat partial_progress as reliable signal
No high-confidence success-like trajectories for preference construction
Ambiguous trajectories dominate, weak memory coverage

observed_outcome.main_failure_remaining: structural misalignment: approach_progress favors early_failure, stability_near penalizes near-pad progress, terminal_penalty harms partial progress
observed_outcome.main_success_signal: success_like trajectories now exist (10 out of 50 in trajectory judgment subset), training return improved from -69.87 to 207.60

failure_repair_outcome.failure_before: structural misalignment: approach_progress favors early_failure over partial progress, stability_near penalizes near-pad progress, terminal_penalty applied to non-crashes
failure_repair_outcome.repair_attempt: Restructured terminal handling into separate crash and landing components, removed standalone stability_near, preserved progress-conditioned gating
failure_repair_outcome.outcome_after: Success-like trajectories emerged (10/50), training return improved, but label margin for partial progress vs early_failure remains negative (progress_gap -0.072, stability_gap -1.49), indicating partial progress not reliably preferred over early_failure

recent_failure_memory_used.lesson: Restructuring terminal handling and removing a penalizing stability component enabled success-like trajectories to appear, but positive label margin must be achieved for partial progress over early failure before preference learning is viable; approach_progress must be recalibrated to favor higher-quality partial progress.
future_guidance: Continue to preserve progress-conditioned gating and low_progress_timeout
Restructure approach_progress component to clearly favor partial_progress over early_failure (current gap negative)
Do not construct success_like preference pairs until label margin is sufficient and success_like trajectories are reliable
Avoid using terminal_penalty that penalizes partial progress; ensure penalty only for crashes
Consider relaxing terminal_bonus conditions to make safe landing more achievable, as suggested by analyzer

archive_update.reason: Candidate is already in elite archive; it improves training return and produces success-like trajectories, but memory coverage remains ambiguous, so continue as parent.

uncertainties: No success_like trajectories in main coverage set (162 trajectories); only 10 observed in smaller trajectory judgment set
Only 5 early_failure trajectories in main coverage, making statistics unreliable
Label margin negative for partial progress vs early_failure: progress_gap -0.072, stability_gap -1.49

Selected candidate's mutation details not fully known; coverage report does not confirm complete removal of stability_near

failure_repair_outcome.failure_before: no success_like, stability_near penalized partial_progress relative to early_failure
failure_repair_outcome.repair_attempt: replaced stability_near with near_stability, added leg_bonus and proximity_bonus, made terminal_reward more achievable
failure_repair_outcome.outcome_after: success_like trajectories generated, but early_failure still present

recent_failure_memory_used.lesson: Making terminal_bonus more achievable and adding leg_bonus/proximity_bonus can convert partial progress into success-like behavior, but early_failure over-reward may still require addressing approach_progress component.
future_guidance: preserve leg_bonus and proximity_bonus
continue to weaken early_failure reward via approach_progress recalibration
ensure terminal_bonus conditions remain flexible
monitor early_failure count to decrease

failure_repair_outcome.failure_before: components stability_near and terminal_reward penalized partial progress; approach_progress not clearly favoring partial over early_failure

recent_failure_memory_used.lesson: Progress-conditioned mutation with component adjustments improved training return and increased success-like trajectories, but terminal_reward and approach_progress still not reliably aligned to favor higher-quality behavior; positive label margin for success_like vs failures now exists, but success_like count is still low for confident preference construction.
future_guidance: Continue to preserve progress-conditioned gating and low_progress_timeout
Restructure approach_progress component to clearly favor partial_progress over early_failure
Consider removing or gating terminal_reward as it may over-reward low-quality trajectories
Strengthen terminal_bonus achievability with moderate speeds and both legs contact
Do not construct success_like preference pairs until success_like trajectories are more numerous and confidence is high
Consider component_recomposition for next mutation to restructure terminal handling and approach progress

failure_repair_outcome.failure_before: structural component misalignment: stability_near penalizes near-pad partial_progress, terminal_penalty applied to non-crashes, approach_progress does not discriminate quality; no success-like trajectories
failure_repair_outcome.repair_attempt: progress_conditioned mutation with component adjustments: preserved approach_progress, leg_bonus, near_stability, proximity_bonus, terminal_bonus, terminal_failure_penalty, terminal_penalty; removed/gated low_progress_survival_gate, stability_near, terminal_reward, ungated_contact_bonus, ungated_survival_bonus
failure_repair_outcome.outcome_after: success_like trajectories emerged (10 out of 50 in trajectory judgment), training return improved to 6.04, label margins sufficient but success_like not yet high-confidence

recent_failure_memory_used.lesson: Progress-conditioned mutations that remove or gate misaligned components (stability_near, terminal_reward) and preserve progress incentives can finally produce success_like trajectories, but the transition from partial_progress to success_like remains fragile; terminal bonus achievability and clear crash penalties must be further refined to make success_like trajectories high-confidence.
future_guidance: preserve progress-conditioned gating and components that favor partial_progress
strengthen terminal_bonus to be reliably achievable with moderate speed and both legs contact
continue to avoid global survival bonus without progress gating
explore component_recomposition to further align terminal handling for high-confidence success_like
consider constructing preference pairs only after success_like confidence improves
